title: Forget the SpaceX IPO: 3 Rock-Solid Dividend Stocks to Build Your Portfolio Around
author: Leo Sun
description: The article advises against investing in SpaceX following its IPO at an expensive 113x revenue valuation while unprofitable. Instead, it recommends three dividend stocks: Realty Income (REIT with 5.2% yield and 135 consecutive dividend raises), Williams Companies (midstream pipeline operator with 3.5% yield and 10-year payout growth streak), and Philip Morris International (tobacco company with 3.2% yield and consistent annual dividend increases despite declining smoking rates).

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Trading at 113x 2025 revenue with unprofitable operations due to losses in space and AI divisions offsetting Starlink profits. Author explicitly recommends avoiding until valuations cool to more reasonable levels.

Recommended as rock-solid dividend stock with 5.2% forward yield, 135 consecutive dividend raises since 1994, 96%+ occupancy rate, and trading at only 14x forward earnings estimate with expected 3-4% AFFO growth.

Recommended dividend stock with 3.5% yield, 10-year consecutive payout increases, handles 30% of U.S. natural gas production, well-positioned for AI/data center growth, and trading at reasonable 15x adjusted EBITDA with expected 13% EPS growth.

Recommended dividend stock with 3.2% forward yield, consistent annual dividend increases since 2008 spin-off, smoke-free products growing 14% organically and representing 43% of revenue, expected 12% EPS growth, and reasonable 22x forward earnings valuation.

title: 3 Stocks to Load Up On Right Now
author: Reuben Gregg Brewer
description: While the S&P 500 trades near all-time highs, three undervalued dividend stocks offer attractive opportunities: United Parcel Service is at an inflection point in its turnaround with a 6% yield, Clorox is repositioning its portfolio with the Gojo acquisition despite CEO departure, and Realty Income provides stable income with a 5.2% yield and diversified property portfolio.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Management confirmed 2026 as inflection point with expected business improvement in H2, attractive 6% dividend yield, and potential for stock appreciation as turnaround progresses despite current investor pessimism.

Despite near-term headwinds (CEO departure, inflation, past hacks), the company has revamped its portfolio and acquired Gojo (leading hand sanitizer brand), providing growth catalyst and expansion into B2B market with attractive 5% yield.

Reliable REIT with 5.2% yield, diversified portfolio of 15,500+ properties, expanding into new areas (asset management, data centers, casinos), and strong commitment to dividend growth makes it attractive for conservative income investors.

title: 3 Boring Dividend Stocks I'd Buy Instead of SpaceX Any Day
author: Jennifer Saibil
description: The author recommends three dividend stocks—Realty Income, Home Depot, and American Express—as safer alternatives to investing in SpaceX's IPO. These companies offer reliable dividends, strong fundamentals, and resilience in an expensive market. Realty Income provides a 5.3% yield with 55+ years of uninterrupted monthly dividends, Home Depot shows growth despite economic headwinds, and American Express demonstrates strong revenue and earnings growth with affluent customer loyalty.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Praised for 98.9% occupancy rate, 55+ years of uninterrupted monthly dividends, 115 consecutive quarters of dividend increases, 5.3% yield, and solid growth strategy with $31 billion in sourced volume.

Demonstrating resilience with 4.8% sales growth and positive comparable sales despite high mortgage rates; expanding through new store openings and strategic acquisitions (Mingledorff's, SRS Distribution) to strengthen market position.

Strong financial performance with 11% revenue growth and 18% EPS growth; fee-based model creates loyalty and recurring revenue; accelerating spend growth and near 100% retention rates; strong appeal to younger demographics.

title: 5 Best Dividend Stocks to Own in Case the AI Trade Ends
author: Justin Pope
description: As the AI boom may eventually fade, investors should diversify portfolios with dividend stocks from non-tech sectors. The article recommends five dividend-paying companies across real estate, fast food, consumer staples, home improvement, and healthcare that offer stable income and long-term growth potential.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Monthly dividend payments with 30+ consecutive years of increases, 5.3% yield, resilient rental income streams, and expansion into diverse property types demonstrate strong income generation and stability.
49 consecutive annual dividend increases approaching Dividend King status, iconic franchise with 45,000+ locations generating steady royalties and fees, recession-resistant business model.

48-year dividend growth streak nearing Dividend King status, trusted consumer brands with consistent demand, 5.2% dividend yield, recent Gojo acquisition expected to boost earnings despite recent challenges.

Strong history of returning cash to shareholders through dividends and buybacks, evergreen housing market, effective e-commerce adaptation using stores as distribution network, currently trading at a dip due to temporary market conditions.

Poised for 50th consecutive annual dividend increase, broad medical product portfolio, recent FDA approval for robotics-assisted surgery, 3.5% dividend yield, trading at less than 14x 2026 earnings with 6-7% projected annualized earnings growth.

title: 135th Common Stock Monthly Dividend Increase Declared by Realty Income
author: Prnewswire
description: Realty Income Corporation (NYSE:O) announced its 135th consecutive dividend increase, raising its monthly common stock dividend to $0.2710 per share from $0.2705 per share, effective July 15, 2026. The increase reflects the company's consistent portfolio performance and reinforces its position as a member of the S&P 500 Dividend Aristocrats with over 31 consecutive years of dividend increases.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company announced its 135th consecutive dividend increase, demonstrating consistent financial performance and shareholder commitment. The dividend raise, coupled with the company's membership in the S&P 500 Dividend Aristocrats and 31+ years of consecutive increases, indicates strong operational durability and reliable growth trajectory.
